justification: All 7 required rules identified with correct invariant classifications. BR-8 (refund window) correctly marked invariant: false. BR-1 explicitly captures cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction. BR-5 covers discount/promotion mutual exclusivity. Multiple ambiguities surfaced (discount precedence, partial fulfillment, refund boundary, timer start point in assumptions). 11 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the 4 minimum. Confidence level of 55 is appropriate given the stated assumptions and unresolved ambiguities.

observations.text: All 7 expected business rules present with correct invariant classifications, plus an additional rule (BR-2: atomic reservation) that strengthens the analysis
Refund window correctly classified as invariant: false with strong justification citing VIP customers, holiday seasons, and regulatory changes
Cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction explicitly captured in BR-1 with clear justification about phantom stock shortages
Discount/promotion mutual exclusivity clearly identified in BR-5 with invariant: true
11 acceptance criteria — nearly triple the minimum — covering both happy and unhappy paths
Timer ambiguity addressed in assumptions section: 'The 15-minute timer starts at checkout initiation, not at first item reservation'
Rich edge cases covering race conditions, concurrent checkouts, and promotion timing
Escalations with concrete options and recommendations demonstrate domain authority

BR-3 (15-minute reservation timeout) marked invariant: false — the rubric expected invariant: true as an inventory management rule. Bird's reasoning (VP could adjust the window) is defensible but diverges from the expected classification
The 'delivery' definition ambiguity (courier scan vs customer confirmation) was not explicitly flagged as an escalation, though the refund boundary ambiguity partially covers this territory
